2604.13666.title: Automatically Inferring Teachers' Geometric Content Knowledge: A Skills Based Approach
2604.13666.paper_summary: Assessing teachers' geometric content knowledge is essential for geometry instructional quality and student learning, but difficult to scale. The Van Hiele model characterizes geometric reasoning through five hierarchical levels. Traditional Van Hiele assessment relies on manual expert analysis of open-ended responses. This process is time-consuming, costly, and prevents large-scale evaluation. This study develops an automated approach for diagnosing teachers' Van Hiele reasoning levels using large language models grounded in educational theory. Our central hypothesis is that integrating explicit skills information significantly improves Van Hiele classification. In collaboration with mathematics education researchers, we built a structured skills dictionary decomposing the Van Hiele levels into 33 fine-grained reasoning skills. Through a custom web platform, 31 pre-service teachers solved geometry problems, yielding 226 responses. Expert researchers then annotated each response with its Van Hiele level and demonstrated skills from the dictionary. Using this annotated dataset, we implemented two classification approaches: (1) retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and (2) multi-task learning (MTL). Each approach compared a skills-aware variant incorporating the skills dictionary against a baseline without skills information. Results showed that for both methods, skills-aware variants significantly outperformed baselines across multiple evaluation metrics. This work provides the first automated approach for Van Hiele level classification from open-ended responses. It offers a scalable, theory-grounded method for assessing teachers' geometric reasoning that can enable large-scale evaluation and support adaptive, personalized teacher learning systems.
2604.13666.paper_summary_zh: 評估教師的幾何內容知識對於幾何教學質量和學生學習至關重要，但難以擴展。Van Hiele 模型通過五個層次來表徵幾何推理。傳統的 Van Hiele 評估依賴於對開放式回答的人工專家分析。這一過程耗時、成本高，並阻礙了大規模評估。本研究開發了一種自動化的方法，利用基於教育理論的大型語言模型來診斷教師的 Van Hiele 推理水平。我們的中心假設是，整合明確的技能信息顯著改善 Van Hiele 分類。與數學教育研究者合作，我們建立了一個結構化的技能詞典，將 Van Hiele 層次分解為 33 種細緻的推理技能。通過一個定制的網絡平台，31 名預備教師解決了幾何問題，產生了 226 個回答。專家研究人員隨後為每個回答標註了其 Van Hiele 層次和詞典中的顯示技能。利用這個標註數據集，我們實施了兩種分類方法：（1）檢索增強生成（RAG）和（2）多任務學習（MTL）。每種方法都比較了包含技能詞典的技能感知變體與不包含技能信息的基線。結果顯示，對於這兩種方法，技能感知變體在多個評估指標上顯著優於基線。這項工作提供了從開放式回答中自動化的 Van Hiele 層次分類的首個方法。它提供了一種可擴展的、基於理論的方法來評估教師的幾何推理，能夠支持大規模評估並促進適應性、個性化的教師學習系統。
2604.13666.author: Ziv Fenigstein et.al.
2604.13666.authors: Ziv Fenigstein, Kobi Gal, Avi Segal, Osama Swidan, Inbal Israel, Hassan Ayoob
